que: In Coroutines, what is the primary goal?
options: skip state/data modeling
replace testing entirely
write async code sequentially
avoid understanding requirements

que: Which API/concept is most central to Coroutines?
options: legacy XML-only patterns unrelated to this topic
random utility class name
manual polling loop for all tasks
CoroutineScope.launch

que: What is a recommended best practice for Coroutines?
options: ignore edge/error states
structured concurrency with coroutine scopes
optimize before verifying correctness
mix unrelated responsibilities in one function

que: Which is a common mistake when implementing Coroutines?
options: keep boundaries clear
follow official docs for API usage
using GlobalScope for app logic
write focused tests for behavior

que: For production code, which approach fits Coroutines best?
options: Use Coroutines and suppress all warnings
Use Coroutines only as theory with no implementation
Use Coroutines with clear state flow and testable logic
Use Coroutines while bypassing architecture boundaries

que: A teammate asks where to start with Coroutines. What is best advice?
options: avoid feedback from tests/review
Read official docs (https://developer.android.com/) and implement a small sample
copy an unknown snippet without context
skip running the app after changes

que: Which statement about Coroutines is most accurate?
options: Coroutines is most effective when paired with clear ownership of state/data
Coroutines removes the need for any testing
Coroutines is mainly about adding boilerplate
Coroutines should be applied identically in every layer

que: In a code review for Coroutines, what would you flag?
options: Code that hides failures instead of handling them explicitly
traceable data flow between layers
explicit modeling of UI/data states
small pure functions with clear names

que: Which scenario is the strongest example of using Coroutines correctly?
options: A small, working feature that demonstrates CoroutineScope.launch in context
an implementation with no error states
a large refactor without tests
a slide deck with no runnable code

que: To learn Coroutines deeply, what should you do next?
options: memorize definitions only
skip verification to save time
avoid official references
Build, test, profile (if needed), and iterate using Coroutines patterns
